notes: agy's !cmd shell-escape runs a local shell with no model round trip. Intent reading (per the claudecode sibling): state must NOT stick in working — no synthesized turn. The antigravity parser has no path that classifies a user shell-escape as a turn, so the shell_escape_window stays ready; a follow-up normal prompt then proves the session is alive with a clean ready→working→ready. EMISSION CAVEAT: no sample contains a !cmd line, so record must confirm the escape is absent from the FILTERED transcript (else a spurious turn would be a daemon bug). Single-birth adapter; tokens/cost absent (SQLite-only).

text: After the !echo hello shell-escape runs, state stays ready — the parser synthesizes no turn for a user shell escape
A normal user prompt drives ready → working — proves the session is still alive
